intent: Kid-uploaded chat images are durable art references that Bit can recall, pass to bot jobs by stable id, and bots can feed into generate_image as reference images without copying into the project workbench.

automated_check.command: pnpm exec vitest run src/main/conversation/conversationService.test.ts src/main/bit/bitCoordinatorService.test.ts src/main/pi/piRuntimeService.test.ts src/main/pi/imageGenTool.test.ts -t "attached picture|stable id|recalls a picture|freshly shared picture|builder reference pictures|known job reference picture|workbench-relative reference"

durable_attachment_storage.source_test: ConversationService > stores an attached picture on disk and keeps its base64 out of the transcript
durable_attachment_storage.observed_behavior: saveAttachment returns metadata with a stable id/path, writes the file under attachments/, and transcript JSON contains the path but not the base64 bytes.

later_recall_by_bit.source_test: BitCoordinatorService > recalls a picture shared in an earlier turn for a later build
later_recall_by_bit.observed_behavior: Bit calls list_builder_pictures on a later turn, extracts the prior picture id, and delegates a build using referencePictureIds.

bot_job_reference_payload.source_test: BitCoordinatorService > hands a builder's freshly shared picture to the bot as an art-direction reference
bot_job_reference_payload.observed_bot_instruction: The bot prompt contains the reference id and tells the bot to pass it in generate_image reference_paths.

bot_access_without_project_copy.source_test: PiRuntimeService > registers the builder's reference pictures for the bot's workbench while it runs, then clears them
bot_access_without_project_copy.observed_behavior: resolveJobReference(cwd, id) returns the factory-level attachment only during the matching bot turn and only for the matching workbench cwd; it is cleared after the turn.

codex_reference_image_input.source_test: createGenerateImageTool > attaches a known job reference picture as an input_image, by id
